column2: Bombard any factories or resources within the given area. Priority will be given to high value targets with low AA protection.
Right-click on the map to select a second target province.
Bombards any infrastructure and supply dumps. Priority will be given to high value targets with low AA protection.
Bombard airfields in an attempt to catch aircraft on the ground. Priority will be given to high value targets with low AA protection.
Attacks radar stations, rocket testing facilities and nuclear facilities. Priority will be given to high value targets with low AA protection.
Patroling an area while looking for moving ground units. If none are found, static targets may be considered.
Patroling an area looking for ground units, with priority given to units in combat.
Patroling in search of enemy aircraft.
Protect the carrier on which it is based from air attack, and bomb any fleets the carrier is fighting.
Perform an airborne assault on a province, dropping paratroopers on the target.
Attempt to locate and destroy naval targets.
Attack an enemy naval base and the ships based there.
Rebasing aircraft to a carrier, which then becomes that air unit's base.
Dropping a nuclear bomb on a target, reducing the enemy's will to fight.
Deliver supplies through the air to any troops needing them.
Transfer the unit to a new home base.
This unit's assets will be used to replace the losses of air units running missions out of this airbase.
Will send the unit to reinforce another unit.
Will scramble to intercept incoming enemy aircraft.
Hunt down hostile convoys at sea and sink them.
Redeploying a unit will make it move faster to its destination, but it costs more supplies and the unit suffers organisation losses while traveling.
Rebasing aircraft to a carrier, which then becomes that air unit's base.
